name: database-orm
description: Interaction with NeonDB Postgres using Drizzle ORM.
qualityReason: Specific ORM with type safety focus
capabilities: Provide guidance on setting up NeonDB Postgres connections with Drizzle ORM
Explain database schema definitions for users, sessions, accounts, and custom tables
Show examples of select, insert, update, and delete operations using Drizzle ORM
Document migration workflows using drizzle-kit for schema changes
Demonstrate type-safe database queries with TypeScript and Drizzle ORM syntax
author: AbdulSamad94
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation for database operations using Drizzle ORM with NeonDB Postgres. Static analysis flagged multiple false positives including C2 keywords (the word drizzle from Drizzle ORM) and cryptographic patterns (md file extensions). The only legitimate risk factor is environment variable access for DATABASE_URL, which is standard practice for database connections. All code examples are TypeScript snippets in documentation, not executable malicious code.
valueStatement: Database operations are complex and error-prone when done manually. This skill provides guidance on using Drizzle ORM with NeonDB Postgres for type-safe database queries, schema management, and migrations.
userTitle: Manage NeonDB Postgres with Drizzle ORM
limitations: Does not execute database operations directly, only provides documentation and guidance
Specific to NeonDB Postgres and Drizzle ORM, not compatible with other databases or ORMs
Requires DATABASE_URL environment variable to be configured by the user
Does not include authentication or authorization logic beyond schema examples

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers setting up new projects
useCases.title: Set Up Database Connection
useCases.description: Learn how to initialize a NeonDB Postgres connection using Drizzle ORM with the serverless driver for your TypeScript application.

useCases.title: Define Database Schemas
useCases.description: Understand how to create type-safe schema definitions for authentication tables and application-specific data models using Drizzle ORM.

useCases.title: Perform Database Queries
useCases.description: Get examples of common database operations including selecting records with filters, inserting new data, and using Drizzle ORM query methods.

promptTemplates.title: Basic: Show Database Connection Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up a NeonDB Postgres connection with Drizzle ORM
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new project and need to configure database access

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Query User Records
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I query the users table to find a user by email using Drizzle ORM?
promptTemplates.scenario: Implementing user lookup functionality in an authentication flow

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Insert New Records
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to insert a new record into the analyses table
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding new application data to the database

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Run Database Migrations
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the workflow for generating and applying schema migrations with drizzle-kit?
promptTemplates.scenario: Updating database schema in production safely

bestPractices: Store database credentials in environment variables, never hardcode connection strings in source code
Use Drizzle ORM type-safe query methods instead of raw SQL to prevent injection vulnerabilities
Generate and test migrations in development before applying them to production databases
antiPatterns: Do not expose DATABASE_URL or other connection strings in client-side code or API responses
Avoid running database migrations directly in production without testing in a staging environment first
Do not bypass Drizzle ORM query builders with raw SQL unless absolutely necessary for complex queries

faq.question: What is NeonDB and why use it?
faq.answer: NeonDB is a serverless Postgres database platform. It provides automatic scaling, branching for development workflows, and pay-per-use pricing. It works well with edge functions and serverless applications.

faq.question: What is Drizzle ORM?
faq.answer: Drizzle ORM is a TypeScript ORM for SQL databases. It provides type-safe database queries, schema definitions, and migrations. It has minimal overhead and generates efficient SQL queries.

faq.question: Do I need to install additional packages?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install three packages: drizzle-orm for the ORM functionality, @neondatabase/serverless for the NeonDB driver, and drizzle-kit for migration tooling.

faq.question: How do I configure the DATABASE_URL?
faq.answer: Get your database connection string from the NeonDB dashboard. Add it to your environment variables file as DATABASE_URL. The format is postgres://user:password@host/database with SSL parameters for NeonDB.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with other databases?
faq.answer: This skill is specific to NeonDB Postgres with Drizzle ORM. Drizzle ORM supports other databases like MySQL and SQLite, but you would need different drivers and connection configurations.

faq.question: How do I handle database migrations?
faq.answer: Use drizzle-kit to generate migration files from schema changes. Run drizzle-kit generate to create migration SQL files, then apply them with drizzle-kit push or a custom migration script in your deployment pipeline.
